guidance: Check turn ownership, async continuation, final delivery, and duplicate-send behavior.
Look for restart/recovery regressions, state cleanup races, and changes in reply semantics.
Check routing, suppression rules, parse/format behavior, and per-channel delivery semantics.
Pay attention to replay, idempotency, and message-loss risk.
Check startup wiring, ingress/recovery ordering, and durable spool/replay behavior.
Check tool result contracts, async delivery, task registry integration, and user-visible tool semantics.
Check transport lifecycle, tool exposure rules, and failure handling.
Check compaction correctness, summary persistence, and context reconstruction behavior.
Check durable task state transitions, terminal state semantics, and projection/reporting contracts.
Check token handling, refresh flows, secret exposure, and persistence of credentials.
Check externally reachable API changes, validation, auth boundaries, and migration compatibility.
Check provider fallback behavior, request/response parsing, and model/tool contract changes.
Check env/config compatibility, default values, and upgrade risk for existing deployments.
Check CLI compatibility and operational behavior changes.
Check CI behavior changes, branch protection expectations, and whether local reviewer guidance needs updating.
Verify tests actually cover the changed behavior and would fail before the change.
